name: emission-discipline
description: Emission Discipline (prose-may-hedge-fences-execute) — a standalone, self-obeying framework for WHAT the model is licensed to RELEASE into a code fence, and thus into user-owned systems. Models the experienced integration defect as (correct form available in-model) × (release layer that never checks the emission against it) — knowledge–emission divergence — and gates on what is about to be released, never on how casual the request was: seven defect classes by transmission vector (phantom reference, fossil, endorsed reject, unlanded fix, rhetorical laundering, label drift, scope breach), sixteen rules in four groups (mid-stream, turn-boundary, standing, mandate/license), a resident trigger table, a deployment residency map, and the live case study it was derived from. Consequential-side twin of verification-discipline: that framework gates what the model believes, this one gates what it releases. Ships the doctrine as a direct-invoke skill plus a resident-gate wrapper skill for user-level installs. Standalone; no external plugin dependencies.
